card.title: 5 Legal Ways to Reduce Your Australian Tax Bill
card.excerpt: Super Contributions, Investment Property Depreciation, Vehicle Expenses, Work-from-Home and Private Health Cover

en.intro: Most tax optimisation is not about clever strategies or grey-area claims. It is about making sure you are using every legitimate entitlement you are already qualified for. Here are five of the most commonly missed opportunities — each legal, ATO-compliant, and available to most working Australians.

sections.title: Extra Super Contributions — Particularly Valuable at Higher Income Levels
paragraphs: Voluntary concessional (before-tax) superannuation contributions are taxed at only 15% inside the fund — regardless of your marginal rate. For someone earning $135,000 (37% marginal rate), each $1 contributed to super saves 22 cents in tax compared to taking the same dollar as salary.
bullets: The 2025-26 concessional contribution cap is $30,000 per year (including your employer's SGC contributions)
If your super balance is under $500,000, you may be able to carry forward unused caps from the past five years
You must submit a Notice of Intent to Claim a Deduction to your super fund before lodging your tax return — without this form, the tax deduction cannot be claimed
Most valuable for individuals earning over $90,000, particularly in the 37–45% tax brackets

sections.title: Investment Property Depreciation — The Deduction Most Landlords Miss
paragraphs: Most investment property owners claim their mortgage interest. Far fewer claim the depreciation deductions they are entitled to. Building depreciation (Div 43) and plant and equipment depreciation (Div 40) — hot water systems, air conditioning, carpets, blinds — are legitimate annual deductions that require a Tax Depreciation Schedule from a Quantity Surveyor.
bullets: Cost of a depreciation schedule: approximately $300–$500 (one-time)
Typical annual depreciation deduction: $3,000–$15,000+ depending on property age and fit-out
The depreciation report is reused every year for the life of the property — it is one of the most cost-effective tax investments available
Note: Do not confuse repairs (immediately deductible) with capital improvements (depreciated over 40 years)

sections.title: Work-Related Vehicle Expenses
paragraphs: Travel between your home and regular workplace is not deductible. But travel between client sites, to a second workplace, to carry heavy equipment, or to attend work-related training can be claimed. There are two methods:

sections.title: Car Expense Methods
paragraphs: Cents per Kilometre Method: 85 cents per kilometre (2025-26 rate) for up to 5,000 km. No receipts required — but you must have a reasonable basis for the distance claimed.

Logbook Method: Keep a continuous log for 12 weeks covering date, destination, purpose, and distance. Apply the business-use percentage to actual annual vehicle costs (fuel, insurance, registration, depreciation). Usually produces a larger deduction for high work-use vehicles.

sections.title: Working From Home — Two Options, Different Outcomes
paragraphs: If you worked from home during the year, you can claim either:
bullets: Fixed Rate Method: 67 cents per hour worked from home. This already includes phone, internet, stationery and consumables — do not also claim these separately.

Actual Cost Method: Claim the actual proportion of electricity, internet, and equipment depreciation used for work purposes. Requires a dedicated work area and detailed records. Often produces a higher deduction for those with a home office.

sections.title: Private Hospital Cover — Avoiding the Medicare Levy Surcharge
paragraphs: Australians earning above $93,000 (single) who do not hold an eligible private hospital policy are subject to the Medicare Levy Surcharge (MLS) of 1.0–1.5% of their taxable income. A basic hospital policy typically costs $1,200–$2,000 per year — often less than the surcharge you would otherwise pay.

sections.title: Medicare Levy Surcharge — Common Mistake
paragraphs: Only hospital cover (not extras/general treatment cover) exempts you from the MLS.
Buying extras cover only (dental, optical, physiotherapy) does not remove the surcharge obligation.
If you are above the threshold and do not have hospital cover, review whether purchasing a basic policy would save you money on net.

takeawayItems: Extra Super Contributions — Particularly Valuable at Higher Income Levels
Investment Property Depreciation — The Deduction Most Landlords Miss

card.title: 澳洲合法减税的 5 种方法
card.excerpt: Super 追加供款、投资房折旧、车辆费用、居家办公与私人住院险

zh.intro: 大多数税务优化不在于聪明的策略或打擦边球——而在于确保你把所有已有资格享受的合法权益都用上了。以下是五种最常被忽略的机会：每种方法都合法、符合 ATO 规定，且大多数在职澳洲人均可使用。

sections.title: 追加 Super 供款——高收入者尤为有价值
paragraphs: 自愿的税前（concessional）超级年金供款在基金内只按 15% 征税——无论你的边际税率是多少。对于年收入 $135,000（边际税率 37%）的人来说，每向 Super 存入 $1，比作为薪水拿到手要少缴 22 分的税。
bullets: 2025-26 财年税前供款上限为每年 $30,000（含雇主 SGC 缴款）
如果你的 Super 余额低于 $500,000，可以利用过去五年的未使用额度结转
必须在提交税表前向 Super 基金提交「意向索取扣除申报表（Notice of Intent to Claim a Deduction）」——没有这份表格，扣税无法申报
对年收入超过 $90,000、尤其是处于 37–45% 税率区间的人最有价值

sections.title: 投资房折旧——大多数房东漏报的扣除
paragraphs: 大多数投资房业主都申报了房贷利息，但只有少数人申报了他们有权享受的折旧扣除。建筑折旧（Div 43）和固定资产折旧（Div 40）——热水器、空调、地毯、百叶窗——是合法的年度扣除项，需要由工程造价师（Quantity Surveyor）出具税务折旧报告。
bullets: 折旧报告费用：约 $300–$500（一次性）
年度折旧扣除额：$3,000–$15,000 以上，具体取决于房产年龄和装修情况
折旧报告在房产持有期内每年重复使用——是性价比最高的税务投资之一
注意：维修（当年全额扣除）和资本改良（40 年折旧）不要混淆

sections.title: 工作相关车辆费用
paragraphs: 从家到固定工作地点的通勤不可申报。但在客户现场之间出行、前往第二个工作地点、携带重型设备、或参加工作相关培训的车费可以申报。有两种方法：

sections.title: 车辆费用申报方法
paragraphs: 每公里费率法（Cents per Kilometre）：2025-26 财年费率为每公里 $0.85，最多申报 5,000 公里。无需保留油费收据，但必须有合理依据支撑所申报的里程数。
行车日志法（Logbook Method）：连续记录 12 周的行车日志，包含日期、目的地、用途和里程。计算业务使用比例后，按该比例申报实际全年车辆费用（油费、保险、注册费、折旧）。对于工作用车比例高的人，通常扣除额更大。

sections.title: 居家办公——两种方法，结果不同
paragraphs: 如果你在本财年有居家工作，可以选择以下两种方法之一：
bullets: 固定费率法：每居家工作小时 $0.67。该费率已包含电话、网络、文具和耗材——不要再单独申报这些费用。
实际成本法：申报真实发生的电费、网费及设备折旧中用于工作的比例。需要有专用工作区域和详细记录。对拥有家庭办公室的人，通常扣除额更高。

sections.title: 私人住院险——避免缴纳 Medicare Levy Surcharge
paragraphs: 年收入超过 $93,000（单身）且未持有合格私人住院保险的澳洲人，须缴纳应税收入 1.0–1.5% 的 Medicare Levy Surcharge（MLS）。一份基础住院险通常每年 $1,200–$2,000——往往少于你本来需要支付的附加税。

sections.title: Medicare Levy Surcharge——常见错误
paragraphs: 只有住院险（Hospital Cover）才能豁免 MLS，附加险（洗牙、配镜、物理治疗等）不能。
只购买附加险不能免除附加税义务。
如果你的收入超过门槛且没有住院险，建议核算一下购买基础住院险净额是否划算。

takeawayItems: 追加 Super 供款——高收入者尤为有价值
投资房折旧——大多数房东漏报的扣除
